title: EccKey double-flush: flush() called by drop, then drop re-calls flush via Drop impl
description: Device::with_ecc_key comment says 'Drop flushes the key via EccKey::drop; no explicit flush needed.' EccKey::flush is pub(crate) and EccKey::Drop calls self.flush(). This path is fine. However, if wolfTPM2_UnloadHandle on a handle that has already been unloaded by a prior error-path flush in create() is not idempotent when the handle value is non-zero but stale, a double-unload could be issued. More specifically: after wolfTPM2_CreateAndLoadKey fails, create() calls UnloadHandle on srk and returns Err — but key was zeroed and never loaded, so no key handle exists. The subsequent EccKey construction is aborted (Err path returns before Ok(Self{...})). No actual double-flush of a live key occurs in the current code. However, flush() on a zeroed WOLFTPM2_KEY (handle == 0) being passed to wolfTPM2_UnloadHandle is undocumented; if wolfTPM treats handle-value 0 as TPM_RH_NULL and that is not a no-op, this could generate a spurious TPM command. Not a guaranteed bug but wolfTPM's contract on handle==0 is not documented in the Rust code or SAFETY comments.
